summary: DHH reviews Walter Isaacson's Elon Musk biography, praising it as both a fine biography and an excellent business book. He highlights Musk's five-step 'algorithm' for shipping products as the book's most valuable contribution. While acknowledging Musk's controversial nature and maniacal work ethic, DHH argues readers can extract practical wisdom without becoming Musk disciples. He suggests most founders would benefit from adding even 10% of Musk's intensity to their approach, while maintaining that calmer work philosophies remain valid.
key_points: Musk's five-step algorithm (question requirements, delete, simplify, accelerate, automate) is a powerful and practical methodology applicable beyond his companies
You can learn from people you wouldn't want to be — extracting wisdom doesn't require becoming a disciple or mirror
Musk's extreme intensity works partly because his missions (energy transition, Mars colonization) motivate people to persevere despite his difficult management style
The modern inability to appreciate elements of a flawed character without swallowing it whole is a cultural loss
Most founders would benefit from adding a fraction of Musk's intensity while still maintaining healthier work philosophies
money_quotes: Like all the best business books, it's not merely an instruction manual, but an inspirational guide too.
His entrepreneurial achievements are as incontestable as his maniacal, even masochistic, commitment to a HARDCORE work ethic.
The latter seems as much motivated by escaping personal demons as by moving the needle. But the needle also does move!
You can absolutely learn from people you wouldn't want to be.
Extracting wisdom from Musk's success does not oblige you to become his disciple or his mirror.
A key reason Musk gets away with his madness is that the missions he pursue can motivate people to persevere in spite of him.
The ability to appreciate, applaud, and even partly appropriate elements of a character you'd never wish to swallow whole.
Most founders and executives would do well to add at least 10% of Musk's intensity to their personal recipe.
key_insight: Musk's five-step algorithm for shipping products is universally applicable wisdom, and leaders can adopt elements of his intensity without embracing the extremes that make it a package deal.
